concentration.headline: Moderately concentrated — one leading city
concentration.body: Lagos leads Nigeria's urban system with 22.9% of tracked urban population across 130 cities — significant but not overwhelming. Secondary cities retain meaningful economic weight alongside the capital.
concentration.comparison: Similar to: United Kingdom, Italy, Canada
concentration.contrast: Unlike: USA, Japan (more distributed) or Thailand, Argentina (more dominant)

gravity.headline: Dense gravitational cluster — Lagos & Ibadan
gravity.body: The strongest gravitational pull exists between Lagos and Ibadan (120 km apart, combined population 20,189,000). Cities form a tight gravitational cluster with top pairs averaging 67 km apart. This dense sub-regional network often functions as a single megalopolitan economy.

narration.synthesis: Nigeria sits between distributed and dominant: Lagos leads with 22.9% but doesn't overwhelm. Gravity clusters tightly around Lagos → Ibadan (67 km avg) — a strong sub-regional core coexists with the national leader, creating a layered urban hierarchy.
